title: Canon XII.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Against those who have given money that they might be entirely undisturbed by evil, an accusation cannot be brought.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For they have sustained the loss and sacrifice of their goods that they might not hurt or destroy their soul, which others for the sake of filthy lucre have not done; and yet the Lord says, “What is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?” and again, “Ye cannot serve God and mammon.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: In these things, then, they have shown themselves the servants of God, inasmuch as they have hated, trodden under foot, and despised money, and have thus fulfilled what is written: “The ransom of a man’s life are his riches.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For we read also in the Acts of the Apostles that those who in the stead of Paul and Silas were dragged before the magistrates at Thessalonica, were dismissed with a heavy fine.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For after that they had been very burdensome to them for his name, and had troubled the people and the rulers of the city, “having taken security,” he says, “of Jason, and of the others, they let them go.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: And the brethren immediately sent away Paul and Silas by night unto Berea.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: After that the saint had finished his discourse concerning those who of their own accord had offered themselves to martyrdom, he said that those were not to be reprehended who by a sum of money paid down freed themselves from the affliction of persecution.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For they preferred to make a sacrifice of their money rather than of their souls.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Then he confirms this, and brings forward different Scripture examples from the Acts of the Apostles concerning the blessed apostle Paul and others.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: But those, he says, are not to be reprehended who have paid money down, and thus escaped, and maintained their piety, nor for this thing may any one bring an accusation against them.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For they have preferred to lose their money rather than their souls, and have shown that they wish to serve God and not mammon; that is, riches.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: And he brings forward the words of Scripture, and the example, as in the Acts of the Apostles, of the blessed apostle Paul and others.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Now, when it is said that they have been undisturbed by all evil, it is to be so taken, either that they have been left undisturbed, so far as the denial of the faith is concerned, which overcomes all evil, or he means the afflictions of persecutions.
